meta.title: Can't Get You Out of My Head
meta.description: An Emotional History of the Modern World — 6 episodes mapping the hidden connections between power, individualism, and conspiracy across the 20th and 21st centuries.

nodes.label: George Boole
nodes.desc: Victorian mathematician who invented Boolean logic — binary system of thought (if/then) that became the foundation of all computing.

nodes.label: Ethel Boole
nodes.desc: George Boole's daughter. Went to Russia, wrote The Gadfly — a revolutionary novel that inspired millions in Russia and China without her knowing.

nodes.label: Geoffrey Hinton
nodes.desc: Boole's great-great-grandson. Solved AI by feeding mass data into neural networks instead of rules. Created 'Vector-World' at Google.

nodes.label: Jiang Qing
nodes.desc: Mao's wife. Used the Cultural Revolution to take revenge on those who'd held back her film career. Hanged herself in 1991 after hoarding socks for a rope.

nodes.label: Mao Zedong
nodes.desc: Used Jiang Qing and the violence she created to destroy rivals, then turned on her: 'grandiose aims but puny abilities.'

nodes.label: Deng Xiaoping
nodes.desc: Replaced revolutionary dreams with money. Used cheap goods as 'the equivalent of opium' to reverse China's century of humiliation.

nodes.label: Xi Jinping
nodes.desc: Created Algorithmic Governance / Social Credit — a system where what goes on inside people's heads is 'completely irrelevant.'

nodes.label: Richard Nixon
nodes.desc: Told a psychiatrist that when he looked in the mirror, 'it was as if there was no-one there.' Created the enemies list. Let go of the gold standard.

nodes.label: Kerry Thornley
nodes.desc: Co-founder of Discordianism. Marine buddy of Oswald. Started Operation Mindfuck to parody conspiracies — then fell victim to one himself.

nodes.label: Greg Hill
nodes.desc: Co-created Operation Mindfuck with Thornley. Became an alcoholic: 'my sheer damn inability to know anything with any deep level of certainty.'

nodes.label: Tupac Shakur
nodes.desc: 'The black prince of the revolution.' Attacked Illuminati conspiracy theories as avoidance. Shot dead in 1996; no one knows who did it.

nodes.label: Afeni Shakur
nodes.desc: Black Panther, Tupac's mother. Suspected an undercover cop in her cell — she was right but no one believed her. Became addicted to crack.

nodes.label: Michael X
nodes.desc: Born Michael de Freitas. Coined 'Englishism.' Revolution collapsed into drug dealing. Murdered a follower. Executed in Trinidad.

nodes.label: Peter Rachman
nodes.desc: Polish Jew. Survived Nazi sterilisation, Russian cannibalism camps, WWII. Became London's most hated landlord — but was the only one housing immigrants.

nodes.label: Daniel Kahneman
nodes.desc: Nobel-winning psychologist. As a child, an SS officer kissed him instead of killing him. Concluded the 'self' is a simplifying accessory to the brain.

nodes.label: B.F. Skinner
nodes.desc: Behaviourist who trained pigeons with rewards. Wrote Walden Two about a utopia of operant conditioning. Prototype for social credit.

nodes.label: Horst Herold
nodes.desc: Invented mass electronic surveillance to catch terrorists. Warned: reform the system or build global surveillance to control the anger.

nodes.label: Horst Mahler
nodes.desc: German revolutionary who found Hitler pictures in a Palestinian training camp. Later became a neo-Nazi.

nodes.label: Andreas Baader
nodes.desc: Red Army Faction leader who declared 'Fucking and shooting are the same thing' at a Palestinian camp.

nodes.label: Gertrude Bell
nodes.desc: Daughter of a baronet. Projected a romantic English dream onto Iraq: 'The sheiks are like great aristocrats.'

nodes.label: Cecil Sharp
nodes.desc: Recorded folk songs and dances to create 'a new kind of English nationalism' based on the idea of the folk — taken from German nationalism.

nodes.label: Saddam Hussein
nodes.desc: Given a list of communists by the CIA. Commissioned a propaganda film directed by the same man who made Dr No.

nodes.label: Terence Young
nodes.desc: British director who made both James Bond's Dr No AND Saddam Hussein's propaganda film about the 1963 coup.

nodes.label: Abu Zubaydah
nodes.desc: Jihadist who loved Chris de Burgh. Shrapnel in the brain fragmented his identity. Waterboarded 83 times; memories from Godzilla became intelligence.

nodes.label: Vladimir Putin
nodes.desc: 'A man who believed in nothing.' Selected by oligarchs. Discovered populist anger as a power source. Became Western liberals' dark mythological villain.

nodes.label: Eduard Limonov
nodes.desc: Poet, provocateur. Founded National Bolsheviks ('fusion of fascism and communism'). Fired a machine gun into Sarajevo. His novel poisoned a generation.

nodes.label: Bo Xilai
nodes.desc: Chinese princeling who sang Maoist songs while allegedly running a corrupt empire. Sent his son to Harrow. Wife murdered Neil Heywood.

nodes.label: Neil Heywood
nodes.desc: Englishman in Beijing. Drove an Aston Martin with plate '007'. Dropped MI6 hints. Found dead in the Lucky Holiday Hotel, Chongqing.

nodes.label: Ewen Cameron
nodes.desc: CIA psychiatrist who ran MKUltra at the Allan Memorial. Used electroshocks and LSD to wipe minds. Failed completely to implant new ones.

nodes.label: Betty Ford
nodes.desc: First Lady who revealed her Valium addiction, exposing 'a private, hidden world of anxiety' across America.

nodes.label: Arthur Sackler
nodes.desc: Marketed Valium as non-addictive. His company later created OxyContin — synthetic opium for post-industrial America.

nodes.label: Bill Clinton
nodes.desc: Gave power to bankers within weeks of entering the White House. 'The era of big government is over.' Brought in mass incarceration.

nodes.label: Tony Blair
nodes.desc: Six historians explained Iraq's complexity. Silence. 'Saddam Hussein is an evil man who needs to be removed.' The historians left.

nodes.label: Dominic Cummings
nodes.desc: Used complexity theory and data to drive Brexit. Saw it as a way to demolish failed elites, not an end in itself.

nodes.label: Jim Garrison
nodes.desc: Created the JFK conspiracy theory using 'Time and Propinquity' — look for patterns, not meaning. Falsely accused Thornley.

nodes.label: Murray Gell-Mann
nodes.desc: Discovered quarks. Promoted Complexity Theory — patterns exist at every level of the universe. Inspired Dominic Cummings.

nodes.label: Julia Grant
nodes.desc: Trans woman whose psychiatrist refused surgery because she was 'not ladylike enough.' Won her fight but ended up alone.

nodes.label: Chai Ling
nodes.desc: Tiananmen student leader who studied Maslow's self-actualisation. Told a journalist she was 'hoping for bloodshed' — then fled.

nodes.label: Bernard Kouchner
nodes.desc: Founded Medecins Sans Frontieres. His rescue of Vietnamese refugees undermined Western liberals' self-belief.

nodes.label: John von Neumann
nodes.desc: Created the pattern of detonations inside the atomic bomb. Built climate models to weaponise weather against the USSR.

nodes.label: Miles Copeland
nodes.desc: Senior CIA officer who openly admitted organising coups worldwide starting with Syria in 1951.

nodes.label: Michael Gazzaniga
nodes.desc: Neuroscientist who studied split-brain patients. Argued humans have multiple selves; the conscious one just makes up stories.

nodes.label: Anna Politkovskaya
nodes.desc: Journalist who said Putin 'has nothing to debate. He has no programme, no personality, no interests.' Shot dead outside her apartment.

nodes.label: Li Lili
nodes.desc: Film star who Jiang Qing believed upstaged her in 1934. Decades later, Jiang Qing had her tortured; her husband killed himself.

nodes.label: Maya Plisetskaya
nodes.desc: Most famous ballerina in the world. Father executed, mother sent to Siberia. Wrote: 'I don't want a cage, even if it is a platinum one.'

nodes.label: Robin Douglas-Home
nodes.desc: Nephew of PM. Married Sandra Paul, insisted she stop modelling. Later caught with the Marchioness of Londonderry. Committed suicide.

nodes.label: Sandra Paul
nodes.desc: Model who married into ruling class. Escaped patriarchal marriage: 'I had to be ruthless in order to be free.'

nodes.label: Patrice Lumumba
nodes.desc: Congo's PM. Killed by CIA because copper was crucial to US electronics. Replaced by Mobutu, whose dictatorship lasted 30 years.

nodes.label: Colonel Mobutu
nodes.desc: Brutal dictator installed by CIA after killing Lumumba. Americans supported his regime for 30 years.

nodes.label: Sir Percy Cradock
nodes.desc: Head of Joint Intelligence Committee. Thought Soviet collapse was fake. Later agreed Hong Kong was never democratic under Britain.

nodes.label: John le Carré
nodes.desc: MI6 spy turned novelist. Understood the secret world was about recreating Empire: 'bug, burgle, assassinate without fear of judgment.'

nodes.label: Anthony Blunt
nodes.desc: Surveyor of the Queen's Pictures. Confirmed he was a Russian spy. Given total immunity to avoid embarrassing the Queen.

nodes.label: Herbert Bayer
nodes.desc: German refugee who designed The Family of Man as 'a new kind of propaganda' — the individual self surrounded by images, making their own story.

nodes.label: Richard Hofstadter
nodes.desc: Political scientist. Argued American paranoia was built into the country from its founding — 'a much larger darkness in the structure of America itself.'

nodes.label: Wang Huning
nodes.desc: China's chief government advisor. Called the corruption 'ultra corruption' — so extensive it was hollowing out the political system.

nodes.label: Gu Kailai
nodes.desc: Bo Xilai's wife, a lawyer. Given a suspended death sentence for poisoning Neil Heywood. Glamorous couple who sent their son to Harrow.

nodes.label: Alexei Navalny
nodes.desc: Internet blogger who chanted the phrase that redefined Russia for an apolitical generation. Arrested; tens of thousands protested.

nodes.label: Jiang Zemin
nodes.desc: Instructed China to buy US government debt — creating 'a safe bubble wrapped around the United States' that funded America's dream world.

nodes.label: David Graeber
nodes.desc: Anthropologist and activist. Died 2020. Described 'the ultimate hidden truth of the world' — a forgotten idea about collective action.

nodes.label: Woodrow Wilson
nodes.desc: President who dreamed of spreading American democracy globally. Congress refused. The dream collapsed into economic depression and race riots.

nodes.label: Rodney King
nodes.desc: Beaten by four LA police officers despite offering no resistance. Officers acquitted by white jury. Six days of riots followed.

nodes.label: Hans Morgenthau
nodes.desc: Named America's hidden system of power 'the Dual State.' Warned the secrecy was 'creating a dangerous time bomb at the heart of America.'

nodes.label: Harry Caudill
nodes.desc: Represented Appalachian miners. Predicted their anger would 'infect all of America.' Shot himself when diagnosed with Parkinson's.

nodes.label: King Faisal
nodes.desc: Used oil as a weapon in 1973. Within three years Saudi Arabia had more foreign currency than Japan, Germany and the US combined.

nodes.label: Vladimir Komarov
nodes.desc: Cosmonaut killed in Soyuz 1. Demanded an open casket — 'his revenge.' Gagarin found hundreds of faults but the Party refused to stop the launch.

nodes.label: Yuri Gagarin
nodes.desc: First man in space. Inspected Komarov's spacecraft, found hundreds of faults, begged them not to launch. They ignored him.

nodes.label: Lin Biao
nodes.desc: Head of People's Army. Mao tricked him into plotting a coup. Fled in a plane that crashed in Mongolia.

nodes.label: Wei Jingsheng
nodes.desc: Electrician at Beijing Zoo. Posted on Democracy Wall calling Deng 'a new fascist dictator.' Arrested, given 15 years.

nodes.label: Stokely Carmichael
nodes.desc: First to articulate 'Black Power' — on a march when MLK was absent. Electrified a generation and terrified white America.

nodes.label: Colonel Mengistu
nodes.desc: Ethiopian tyrant who used Live Aid food as a weapon. Forced mass relocations that killed 100,000 people.

nodes.label: Bob Geldof
nodes.desc: Live Aid organiser. Horrified when he discovered Colonel Mengistu was using food aid as a weapon of mass relocation.

nodes.label: Peter Mair
nodes.desc: Political scientist who argued democracy didn't collapse — it was quietly abandoned by politicians who 'changed their roles' in the 1990s.

nodes.label: Tony Martin
nodes.desc: Norfolk farmer who shot burglars. Uncle was a League of Empire Loyalist. Linked Brexit-era anger to post-imperial conspiracy.

nodes.label: Edward Lorenz
nodes.desc: Discovered Chaos Theory by accident — a rounding error in a climate model produced wildly different results.

nodes.label: Yegor Letov
nodes.desc: Musician. Third member of Limonov's National Bolsheviks. Led opposition to communism in the 1980s. Sang of Russia trapped in frozen fragments.

nodes.label: Edgar Mittelholzer
nodes.desc: Novelist from British Guiana. Wrote about colonial ghosts. Listened to Wagner every evening. Poured paraffin over himself and burned to death.

nodes.label: Enoch Powell
nodes.desc: Made violent anti-immigrant speech but wasn't charged — while Michael X was jailed for 10 months for similar words.

nodes.label: Fareed Zakaria
nodes.desc: Political scientist who asked: 'The American people are not stupid — but what if they are?'

nodes.label: Giandomenico Majone
nodes.desc: EU technocrat who designed 'non-majoritarian institutions' — bureaucracies 'by design not directly accountable to voters.'

nodes.label: Joan Baez
nodes.desc: Attacked by Jane Fonda for supporting Kouchner's rescue of Vietnamese refugees — accused of being 'an unwitting agent of American imperialism.'

nodes.label: Richard Holbrooke
nodes.desc: Clinton's Balkans representative. Posed the dilemma: what if free elections elect racists and fascists?

nodes.label: Boris Yeltsin
nodes.desc: 'Drunk most of the time.' Puppet of the oligarchs. Shock therapy created chaos. Limonov emerged as his most unlikely opponent.

nodes.label: John Birch Society
nodes.desc: Extreme right group that spread the theory that Eisenhower was 'a dedicated, conscious agent of the Russians.' Fuelled suburban paranoia.

nodes.label: Night Wolves
nodes.desc: Extreme nationalist motorcycle club who became Putin's bodyguards. Staged mass shows dramatising Illuminati/Obama conspiracy theories.

nodes.label: Wen Jiabao
nodes.desc: Chinese Premier who warned: 'Unless you have political reform and open up the society for democratic debate, those ghosts will always threaten.'

nodes.label: Tang Na
nodes.desc: Jiang Qing's first husband, a famous writer. She left him. He tried to commit suicide by jumping in a river but 'the water was very cold, so he jumped up again.'

nodes.label: Cultural Revolution
nodes.desc: Jiang Qing's weapon and prison. Unleashed anger and violence that China had buried for centuries. Mass killings, suicides, thought crimes.

nodes.label: Tiananmen Square
nodes.desc: 1989. Chai Ling called for bloodshed then fled. Deng sent in tanks. Became the foundation for China's 'Century of Humiliation' propaganda.

nodes.label: Iraq Invasion (2003)
nodes.desc: Six historians warned Blair. He ignored them. Cost $1B/day. Americans repeated the British mistake of empowering tribes over cities.

nodes.label: September 11 Attacks
nodes.desc: 'We failed to join up the dots.' Led to Patriot Act, which removed obstacles to Google's data collection. Privacy became irrelevant.

nodes.label: 2008 Financial Crash
nodes.desc: Algorithms cut up toxic mortgages until no one could see the absurdity. Governments bailed out banks, transferred debt to the public.

nodes.label: Hong Kong Handover
nodes.desc: Chinese sang Rod Stewart to departing British. Sir Percy Cradock agreed: Britain had never allowed democracy in Hong Kong.

nodes.label: Opium Wars
nodes.desc: Britain forced opium onto China, received silver in return. One historian: 'the British Empire was rotten at its heart.'

nodes.label: LA Riots (1992)
nodes.desc: Six days after Rodney King verdict. 'Fuck everybody, I'm off.' National Guard deployed. Nothing had really changed since the 1960s.

nodes.label: Kursk Submarine Disaster
nodes.desc: Putin discovered populist anger as a power source by blaming the oligarchs for the deaths of 118 sailors.

nodes.label: Birth of a Nation (1915)
nodes.desc: Film that caused a sensation. KKK copied its costumes — which the novelist had invented from Walter Scott's fiction.

nodes.label: Watergate
nodes.desc: Nixon's own conspiracy exposed. In its wake, CIA assassinations, MKUltra, and other real conspiracies leaked out.

nodes.label: Brexit
nodes.desc: Cummings used complexity theory. But the campaign awakened ghosts from Britain's imperial past. 'Take back control' meant different things to different people.

nodes.label: The Awakening (Iraq)
nodes.desc: US paid tribal militias to fight al-Qaida. When the money stopped, the tribes allied with the jihadists. ISIS was born.

nodes.label: Asian Financial Crisis (1997-98)
nodes.desc: Western banks looted Asian economies. IMF loans were used to extract more money. Confirmed Chinese paranoia about Western conspiracy.

nodes.label: Dot-Com Crash
nodes.desc: $100K computers auctioned for 10 cents on the dollar. Google survived but venture capitalists took control. The search for profit began.

nodes.label: Tulsa Massacre (1921)
nodes.desc: White mob attacked black areas, even using aircraft to drop bombs. Out of this fear came the modern KKK.

nodes.label: Entebbe Hijacking
nodes.desc: German anti-fascists helped Palestinians hijack a plane and separated Israeli from non-Israeli passengers — 'behaving exactly as the Nazis had done.'

nodes.label: Panther 21 Trial
nodes.desc: Three founding members of the Black Panther cell were undercover police — the most active members. All 21 acquitted after Afeni's defence.

nodes.label: Birmingham Six Release
nodes.desc: Held for 16½ years. Establishment knew they were innocent: 'an unshakeable conviction that the establishment must never be shown to be wrong.'

nodes.label: Congo Coup / Lumumba
nodes.desc: CIA killed Lumumba because Congo's copper was crucial to US electronics. Installed Mobutu for 30 years. Slave trade origins came full circle.

nodes.label: Oil Crisis (1973)
nodes.desc: King Faisal raised oil prices 4x. Petrodollars flooded Western banks, creating international currency free of government control.

nodes.label: Mau Mau / Kenya Camps
nodes.desc: British put hundreds of thousands of Kenyans in camps for 'psychological adjustment.' Chief psychologist aimed to manipulate 'the African mind.' Mass torture.

nodes.label: Soyuz 1 Disaster
nodes.desc: Komarov died because Party insisted launch go ahead despite hundreds of faults. Demanded open casket as revenge.

nodes.label: Democracy Wall
nodes.desc: Deng allowed brief free expression in Beijing. Wei Jingsheng called him a fascist dictator. Wall shut down, Wei arrested.

nodes.label: Live Aid
nodes.desc: Geldof raised millions for Ethiopia. Mengistu used food as weapon. MSF estimated aid caused as many deaths as it saved.

nodes.label: Camp Century / Project Iceworm
nodes.desc: US base in Greenland hiding 600 nuclear missiles under ice. Ice cores revealed 10-degree temperature shifts in centuries.

nodes.label: Kosovo Intervention
nodes.desc: Blair persuaded reluctant Clinton to bomb Serbia. Kouchner became ruler of independent Kosovo. Politicians reclaimed moral purpose.

nodes.label: Algerian Election Crisis
nodes.desc: Islamic Salvation Front won. Western politicians supported a military coup to stop democracy producing 'the wrong kind of result.'

nodes.label: The Second Empire
nodes.desc: Anti-corruption investigators' name for the City of London's offshore network. Hundreds of billions laundered through secretive territories.

nodes.label: Boolean Logic
nodes.desc: Binary system: if/then, 0/1. Born from imperial desire to map and control. Became the foundation of all computers and algorithms.

nodes.label: Operation Mindfuck
nodes.desc: Fake Illuminati conspiracy seeded into counterculture via Playboy. Meant to parody conspiracies; instead became the template for real ones.

nodes.label: Individualism
nodes.desc: Central ideology of the modern age. Curtis argues it promised liberation but delivered isolation and made people easier to manage.

nodes.label: Chaos Theory
nodes.desc: Tiny changes cause massive unpredictable consequences. Edward Lorenz discovered it by accident. Rose up as the Soviet Union collapsed.

nodes.label: Complexity Theory
nodes.desc: Computers can see hidden patterns humans can't. Murray Gell-Mann. Influenced Cummings. Foundation of today's data-dominated world.

nodes.label: Social Credit System
nodes.desc: China's Algorithmic Governance. Skinner's pigeons at national scale. Rewards for good behaviour; re-education for bad.

nodes.label: The Dual State
nodes.desc: Hans Morgenthau's term: America publicly promotes democracy while secretly running coups. 66 attempted, 26 succeeded.

nodes.label: MKUltra
nodes.desc: CIA mind control programme. Electroshocks and LSD to wipe memories. Failed completely. Patients left as aliens in their own world.

nodes.label: Yellow Peril
nodes.desc: Hysteria about Chinese revenge despite fewer than 1,000 Chinese in Britain. Guilt over opium mutated into fear. Created Dr Fu Manchu.

nodes.label: Century of Humiliation
nodes.desc: Deng Xiaoping's propaganda narrative: the West has been conspiring to destroy China for 150 years, starting with opium.

nodes.label: Exceptionalism (US/UK/Russia)
nodes.desc: Dreamlike myths about national specialness, rooted in fear. American, British, and Russian versions all serve to avoid facing imperial violence.

nodes.label: Nudge / Priming Theory
nodes.desc: Idea that unconscious triggers shape behaviour. Kahneman promoted it; then warned of 'a train wreck looming' when experiments failed replication.

nodes.label: Non-Majoritarian Institutions
nodes.desc: Giandomenico Majone's invention: bureaucracies 'by design not directly accountable to voters.' The quiet dismantling of democracy.

nodes.label: Englishism
nodes.desc: Michael X's term for the hidden ruthlessness behind English politeness — 'an anger and melancholy at the loss of their empire.'

nodes.label: Learned Helplessness
nodes.desc: Positive psychology concept. CIA psychologists reversed it — used it to break Abu Zubaydah through waterboarding and freezing.

nodes.label: Vector-World
nodes.desc: Geoffrey Hinton's name for the reality seen by neural networks — patterns across time and space that humans can never see or understand.

nodes.label: Time and Propinquity
nodes.desc: Jim Garrison's method: don't look for meaning, look for patterns and coincidences. Profoundly shaped conspiracy thinking.

nodes.label: Paranoid Style in America
nodes.desc: Hofstadter: American paranoia is built into the country from its founding — settlers fled corruption but brought their suspicious minds.

nodes.label: Petrodollars
nodes.desc: After 1973 oil crisis, Western banks turned Arab oil money into international currency free of government control. Created new financial power.

nodes.label: Ultra Corruption
nodes.desc: Wang Huning's term for corruption so extensive it was hollowing out China's political system. Risked fragmenting into 1920s-era warlordism.

nodes.label: Shock Therapy
nodes.desc: Sudden introduction of free-market capitalism in Russia. Created chaos, oligarch rule, and mass impoverishment. Poisoned Russians against democracy.

nodes.label: Self-Actualisation (Maslow)
nodes.desc: Maslow's idea that individuals should fulfil their highest potential. Influenced Chai Ling at Tiananmen. Became the ideology of consumer capitalism.

nodes.label: Dream of the Red Chamber
nodes.desc: China's most famous novel — about how those in power shape reality. Jiang Qing was fascinated by it. Used to justify her control of culture.

nodes.label: Community Action
nodes.desc: White professionals surveying Notting Hill residents. Looked like empowerment but was really management through data collection.

nodes.label: Notting Hill
nodes.desc: Rachman's empire. Michael X's territory. Where revolution became consumerism. Where white radicals danced to black music.

nodes.label: Baghdad / Iraq
nodes.desc: Created by the British from romantic dreams. Destabilised by CIA. Invaded twice. Bell's fantasy became ISIS's nightmare.

nodes.label: Chongqing
nodes.desc: 32 million people. Bo Xilai's laboratory. Mass Maoist rallies while his wife allegedly murdered an Englishman at the Lucky Holiday Hotel.

nodes.label: Brighton Beach, NY
nodes.desc: Where the Soviets dumped Russian criminals (calling them Jewish emigrés). The Potato Bag Gang: start of the Russian mafia in America.

nodes.label: Allan Memorial Hospital
nodes.desc: Montreal. Where Ewen Cameron ran MKUltra experiments. Electroshocks, LSD, erased minds. 'I felt like an alien from another world.'

nodes.label: Silicon Valley
nodes.desc: Idealists who believed individuals could remake the world by bypassing old power. Then met the power of money after the dot-com crash.

nodes.label: Saudi Arabia
nodes.desc: 'A fairy-tale land.' No tax. Abu Zubaydah discovered that if you weren't part of the elite, you were nothing. Money created nihilism.

nodes.label: Sarajevo
nodes.desc: Where Limonov fired a machine gun as Karadzic's guest and discovered nationalism as a force against the 'geopolitics of money.'

nodes.label: Tiananmen Square
nodes.desc: Where Chai Ling called for bloodshed. Where Deng sent tanks. Where an anonymous poem called Jiang Qing insane.

nodes.label: Peshawar
nodes.desc: Afghan border town. Where Abu Zubaydah went to lose himself in jihad. Where shrapnel pierced his brain and shattered his identity.

nodes.label: Congo
nodes.desc: Centre of slave trade for 200 years. Copper mines crucial to US electronics. Lumumba killed, Mobutu installed. American empire continuing European instability.

nodes.label: Kenya
nodes.desc: Last outpost of Empire. Mau Mau camps. British tried to manipulate 'the African mind' through psychological adjustment. Mass torture.

nodes.label: Hong Kong
nodes.desc: Never democratic under British rule. Handed back to China in pouring rain. Chinese sang Rod Stewart. Percy Cradock agreed with Chinese criticism.

nodes.label: Appalachia
nodes.desc: Harry Caudill represented miners whose jobs disappeared. Predicted the anger would infect all of America. The forgotten heartland.

nodes.label: The Gadfly (novel)
nodes.desc: Ethel Boole's novel about revolutionary sacrifice. Inspired millions in Russia and China without her knowledge.

nodes.label: Dr No (film)
nodes.desc: James Bond film directed by Terence Young — who also directed Saddam Hussein's propaganda film.

nodes.label: Walden Two (novel)
nodes.desc: Skinner's utopia of operant conditioning. Became the blueprint for China's social credit system.

nodes.label: The Gulag Archipelago
nodes.desc: Solzhenitsyn's conclusion: stop trying to change the world. 'The safest thing to do in the future was to believe in nothing.'

nodes.label: It's Me, Eddie (novel)
nodes.desc: Limonov's novel about America's hidden reality. Every publisher refused it. When published in Russia, it poisoned a generation against democracy.

nodes.label: The Clansman (novel)
nodes.desc: Invented KKK costumes from Walter Scott's fiction. DW Griffith adapted it into Birth of a Nation. The real KKK copied the film.

nodes.label: Killuminati (album)
nodes.desc: Tupac's posthumous attack on Illuminati conspiracy theories: 'Suspicion is just another form of control.'

nodes.label: Bloodshed on Wolf Mountain
nodes.desc: 1934 Chinese film. Li Lili upstaged Jiang Qing. Decades later, Jiang Qing took her revenge through the Cultural Revolution.

nodes.label: The Immortal Hour (opera)
nodes.desc: Rutland Boughton's national sensation. Set in a dark wood with ancient forces — 'the lordly ones.' Epitome of English folk myth.

nodes.label: Voynich Manuscript
nodes.desc: Mysterious book in an undeciphered language. Inherited by Ethel Boole from her husband. No one has ever cracked it.

nodes.label: The Idle Warriors (novel)
nodes.desc: Thornley's novel with Oswald as protagonist. Written before the assassination. Led Garrison to accuse him of conspiracy.

nodes.label: The Family of Man (exhibition)
nodes.desc: 1950s exhibition of individuals worldwide. Designed by Herbert Bayer as 'a new kind of propaganda' for the individual self.

nodes.label: Dr Fu Manchu
nodes.desc: 'A yellow Satan.' Created by Sax Rohmer from Yellow Peril hysteria. Projection of British guilt over opium onto a Chinese villain.

nodes.label: My Bones and My Flute
nodes.desc: Mittelholzer's novel about colonial ghosts haunting the living. He became a colonial ghost himself — burned to death.

nodes.label: Night Comes to the Cumberlands
nodes.desc: Harry Caudill's book about Appalachian miners. Predicted their anger would 'infect all of America.'

nodes.label: Lee Harvey Oswald
nodes.desc: Marine who befriended Kerry Thornley, defected to the USSR, then returned to assassinate JFK. His story became the template for modern conspiracy theories.

nodes.label: Donald Trump
nodes.desc: Used the conspiracy theories and distrust of institutions that had been building for decades to ride to power. His supporters created QAnon.

nodes.label: John F. Kennedy
nodes.desc: His assassination in 1963 shattered American certainty and launched modern conspiracy culture. Jim Garrison investigated his death.

nodes.label: Alexander Solzhenitsyn
nodes.desc: Soviet dissident who secretly wrote The Gulag Archipelago, exposing the horror of the Soviet prison system. His work helped delegitimise the USSR.

nodes.label: Bobby Seale
nodes.desc: Co-founder of the Black Panther Party. Afeni Shakur heard him speak in Harlem and joined the movement.

nodes.label: Hanns-Martin Schleyer
nodes.desc: Leading German industrialist kidnapped and killed by the RAF. He had also been a leading Nazi — embodying how post-war Germany never fully reckoned with its past.

nodes.label: Yelena Shchapova
nodes.desc: Eduard Limonov's great love. Their glamorous life in Moscow's underground ended when they emigrated. She left him in New York, devastating him.

nodes.label: Malcolm X
nodes.desc: Civil rights leader who influenced Michael X (who took his name) and the Black Panthers. Advocated self-defence over non-violence.

nodes.label: Osama bin Laden
nodes.desc: Ran a jihad camp near Abu Zubaydah's. Asked Zubaydah to form an alliance, but Zubaydah refused because he didn't trust bin Laden's ambitions.

nodes.label: Steve Yeates
nodes.desc: Michael X's enforcer in Trinidad. Told that Gale Benson was a CIA agent, he murdered her and buried her in the garden.

nodes.label: Keswick Family
nodes.desc: Ran Jardine Matheson, key to the British opium trade with China. William Keswick was caught using insider information in the City of London.

nodes.label: Chris de Burgh
nodes.desc: Irish singer whose music Abu Zubaydah loved, especially 'Lady in Red.' A surreal detail in the story of a man who became the War on Terror's most tortured prisoner.

nodes.label: Ayn Rand
nodes.desc: Libertarian novelist whose ideas of radical individualism influenced Kerry Thornley and shaped American anti-government sentiment.

nodes.label: George W. Bush
nodes.desc: Led the invasion of Iraq in 2003 to bring stability to the Middle East, which instead created chaos and ultimately ISIS.

nodes.label: Margaret Thatcher
nodes.desc: Supported British intelligence throughout the Cold War. Was shocked when they completely failed to predict the collapse of the Soviet Union.

nodes.label: Gale Benson
nodes.desc: English socialite who followed her lover Hakim Jamal to Trinidad, where Michael X ordered her murder, claiming she was a CIA spy.

nodes.label: Hakim Jamal
nodes.desc: Black Power activist who had an affair with Jean Seberg. Took Gale Benson to Trinidad where she was murdered by Michael X's men.

nodes.label: Jean Seberg
nodes.desc: American actress targeted by FBI COINTELPRO for supporting the Black Panthers. The FBI destroyed her reputation and she eventually killed herself.

nodes.label: Barack Obama
nodes.desc: Russian conspiracy theories cast him as part of an Illuminati plot to undermine Putin. His presidency deepened American political polarisation.

nodes.label: OxyContin Epidemic
nodes.desc: Synthetic opium marketed by Arthur Sackler's company as non-addictive — just as he'd marketed Valium. Devastated Appalachia, completing the opium circle from China to America.

nodes.label: Valium
nodes.desc: Marketed by Arthur Sackler as safe treatment for anxiety. Became most prescribed drug in America. Betty Ford's addiction exposed its dangers. Template for OxyContin.

nodes.label: Black Panther Party
nodes.desc: Revolutionary organisation that believed armed self-defence was the only way to stop racism. FBI infiltrated and destroyed them through COINTELPRO.

nodes.label: Gang of Four
nodes.desc: Jiang Qing and three allies who tried to seize power when Mao died. Arrested in 1976, ending the Cultural Revolution and Jiang Qing's dream.

nodes.label: Vietnam War
nodes.desc: Shattered American confidence and fuelled the counterculture. Nixon's secret bombing and the chaos at home drove the turn to individualism and conspiracy thinking.

nodes.label: Cold War
nodes.desc: The ideological struggle that framed individualism as the West's answer to collectivism. Produced MKUltra, the space race, and the surveillance state.

nodes.label: War on Terror
nodes.desc: Used Abu Zubaydah's tortured confessions to justify global surveillance and military intervention. The torture techniques came from MKUltra's Ewen Cameron.

nodes.label: Ku Klux Klan
nodes.desc: Revived in the 1920s using imagery from Walter Scott novels and D.W. Griffith's Birth of a Nation. Its costumes came from romantic fiction about medieval knights.

nodes.label: Red Army Faction (RAF)
nodes.desc: West German urban guerrilla group founded by Andreas Baader and others. Killed Schleyer, hijacked planes, and ultimately discredited left-wing revolution.

nodes.label: Cambridge Analytica
nodes.desc: Claimed to alter behaviour through Facebook data and priming. But the Facebook emotion experiments suggested priming effects were actually tiny.

nodes.label: Facebook Emotion Experiments
nodes.desc: In 2014, Facebook psychologists showed they could alter emotions through newsfeeds. But the effect was only 0.001% — raising doubts about all nudge/priming theory.

nodes.label: JFK Assassination
nodes.desc: The event that shattered American certainty. Jim Garrison's investigation, Oswald's connections to Thornley, and the Warren Commission spawned modern conspiracy culture.

nodes.label: COINTELPRO
nodes.desc: FBI programme to infiltrate and destroy the Black Panthers and other radical groups. Agents provocateurs, forged letters, and psychological warfare.

nodes.label: The Illuminati
nodes.desc: Originally a real 18th-century Bavarian secret society. Greg Hill and Kerry Thornley spread fake Illuminati conspiracy letters as a joke — which people eventually believed.

nodes.label: Vory v Zakone
nodes.desc: 'Thieves in law' — Russian criminal brotherhood with its own codes, born in the prisons. When the Soviet Union collapsed, they merged with the new oligarchs and then with Putin's state.

nodes.label: QAnon
nodes.desc: Conspiracy theory explaining why Trump hadn't fulfilled his promises: a secret cabal was stopping him. Direct descendant of the fake Illuminati letters from Operation Mindfuck.

nodes.label: Neoliberalism
nodes.desc: The idea that markets should replace democratic politics. Giandomenico Majone's 'non-majoritarian institutions' gave it intellectual cover. Led to technocratic governance.

nodes.label: LSD
nodes.desc: Used by Ewen Cameron in MKUltra experiments at Allan Memorial Hospital. Massive doses combined with electroshock to try to reprogram human minds.

nodes.label: Afghanistan
nodes.desc: Where jihadists inspired by Bernard Kouchner's humanitarian interventionism came to fight. Abu Zubaydah ran a logistics camp for foreign fighters near Peshawar.

nodes.label: Trinidad
nodes.desc: Michael X's homeland. He returned there from London, established a commune, and descended into violence — ordering the murder of Gale Benson.

nodes.label: Abu Ghraib
nodes.desc: Iraqi prison where American torture techniques — derived from MKUltra via Abu Zubaydah's interrogation — were used on detainees.

nodes.label: Guantanamo Bay
nodes.desc: Where Abu Zubaydah was held and tortured. He remains there decades later, never charged, because his trial would expose what was done to him.
